basic_details.organization_name: Baba Farid University of Health Sciences (BFUHS)
basic_details.short_information: Baba Farid University of Health Sciences (BFUHS) Ombudsperson Notification 2026 has been released on 09 June 2026 on the official website of BFUHS. A total of 1 vacancy has been announced through the BFUHS Ombudsperson Recruitment 2026.

age_limit.age_details: Maximum age of 70 years at the time of appointment.
age_limit.details: Maximum age: 70 years at the time of appointment.

qualification.education_qualification: Retired Vice-Chancellor or retired Professor (with 10 years experience as Professor at State/Central Universities/Institutions of National Importance/Deemed Universities) or former District Judge.
qualification.details: Retired Vice-Chancellor Retired Professor (with 10 years experience as Professor at State/Central Universities/Institutions of National Importance/Deemed Universities) Former District Judge

salary_details.pay_scale: Sitting fee per diem and reimbursement of conveyance expenses.
salary_details.details: Sitting fee, per diem, in accordance with the norms fixed by the respective university. Reimbursement of the expenditure incurred on conveyance.

selection_process: Appointment based on eligibility and terms & conditions.
how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online on or before 29-06-2026.
To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section.
Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of BFUHS to complete the application.
Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details.
Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format.
Pay the application fee online, if applicable.
Review all details carefully before submitting the form.
Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the BFUHS Ombudsperson recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit your online application for the Ombudsperson post is 29 June 2026. Ensure all details are accurate before the deadline.

faq.question: Q2. What are the minimum qualifications required for the Ombudsperson position?
faq.answer: Candidates must be a retired Vice-Chancellor, a retired Professor with 10 years of experience, or a former District Judge to be eligible for this role.

faq.question: Q3. Is there any age limit for the Ombudsperson post?
faq.answer: Yes, the maximum age limit for the Ombudsperson position is 70 years at the time of appointment.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application mode for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The application process for the BFUHS Ombudsperson recruitment is strictly online. No other mode of application will be accepted.

faq.question: Q5. How will the selection be made for the Ombudsperson role?
faq.answer: The selection will be based on the eligibility of the candidates and the terms and conditions set by the university.

faq.question: Q6. What is the nature of the job for the Ombudsperson post?
faq.answer: The Ombudsperson position is offered on a part-time basis.

faq.question: Q7. What is the salary structure for the Ombudsperson?
faq.answer: The compensation includes a sitting fee per diem and reimbursement of conveyance expenses as per university norms.

faq.question: Q8. Where can I find the official notification for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The official notification PDF can be accessed through the 'Important Links' section on this page, which will provide comprehensive details.

faq.question: Q9. Does BFUHS offer any age relaxation for this post?
faq.answer: The provided data does not specify any age relaxation. Candidates are advised to check the official notification for details.

faq.question: Q10. What is the total number of vacancies for the Ombudsperson post?
faq.answer: There is only one vacancy announced for the Ombudsperson position in this recruitment drive.
